subject: Mathematics
title: Al-Khwarizmi: Father of Algebra
description: Learn Al-Khwarizmi: Father of Algebra in one minute.

hook.text: The word 'algorithm' comes from one man's name. The word 'algebra' comes from his book's title.

buildup.text: Al-Khwarizmi worked in Baghdad's House of Wisdom around 820 AD, translating and extending Greek and Indian math.

discovery.text: His book Al-Jabr introduced systematic methods for solving equations—what we now call algebra.

twist.text: He wrote everything in words. Mathematical symbols wouldn't be invented for another 700 years.

climax.text: His methods spread to Europe through Latin translations and became the foundation of modern math.

punchline.text: Two everyday words carry one medieval scholar's legacy.

quiz.question: Which two common words trace back to Al-Khwarizmi?
